rulings.Case Title: BIANA CHERNOGUZ VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing On Application For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 5, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER BIANA CHERNOGUZ'S Notice Of Hearing On Application For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is DENIED. No abuse of discretion shown. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU ET AL VS. TINA YAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 5, 2021 line 2. rulings.Case Title: BIANA CHERNOGUZ ET AL VS. LEANA PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And Injunction To Enjoin Defendants From Using Fraudulent Declaration As Evidence To Prove The Truth Of The Matters Stated Therein
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 5, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFFS BIANA CHERNOGUZ, VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ'S Motion For Protective Order And Injunction To Enjoin Defendants From Using Fraudulent Declaration As Evidence To Prove The Truth Of The Matters Stated Therein is DENIED without prejudice to raise the issues addressed in this motion at trial or any other fact-based proceeding. Defendant's Request for sanctions granted in the amount of $2,587 to be paid within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)
